definitions.subtitle: ={{$parameter["mode"]}}
definitions.description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available

builderHint.searchHint: Mode selection is the single most consequential decision on this node — the wrong mode silently drops or duplicates items rather than erroring. Pick by data shape: `append` to concatenate items from parallel branches; `combineByPosition` only when both branches emit the same number of items in the same order; `combineByFields` to join by a matching key (default; usually correct for "merge by ID"); `combineBySql` for >2 inputs or aggregation; `chooseBranch` to discard all but one input. Read each mode's @builderHint before picking.

extraTypeDefContent.content:
<patterns>
<pattern title="Combine two branches by matching key (combineByFields, default)">
const usersApi = node({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest',
  version: 4.2,
  config: { name: 'Fetch Users', parameters: { url: 'https://api.example.com/users' } }
});

const ordersApi = node({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest',
  version: 4.2,
  config: { name: 'Fetch Orders', parameters: { url: 'https://api.example.com/orders' } }
});

const mergeNode = merge({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.merge',
  version: 3.2,
  config: {
    name: 'Merge by ID',
    parameters: {
      mode: 'combine',
      combineBy: 'combineByFields',
      fieldsToMatchString: 'id',
      joinMode: 'keepMatches',
      outputDataFrom: 'both'
    }
  }
});

// Wire each upstream branch to a specific merge input slot.
usersApi.to(mergeNode.input(0));
ordersApi.to(mergeNode.input(1));
</pattern>

<pattern title="Append items from parallel branches (append)">
const branchA = node({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.set',
  version: 3.4,
  config: { name: 'Branch A', parameters: {} }
});

const branchB = node({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.set',
  version: 3.4,
  config: { name: 'Branch B', parameters: {} }
});

const mergeNode = merge({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.merge',
  version: 3.2,
  config: {
    name: 'Append',
    parameters: { mode: 'append', numberInputs: 2 }
  }
});

branchA.to(mergeNode.input(0));
branchB.to(mergeNode.input(1));
</pattern>

<pattern title="Three or more inputs with SQL (combineBySql)">
const mergeNode = merge({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.merge',
  version: 3.2,
  config: {
    name: 'SQL Merge',
    parameters: {
      mode: 'combineBySql',
      numberInputs: 3,
      query: 'SELECT * FROM input1 LEFT JOIN input2 ON input1.id = input2.userId LEFT JOIN input3 ON input1.id = input3.userId'
    }
  }
});

input1Node.to(mergeNode.input(0));
input2Node.to(mergeNode.input(1));
input3Node.to(mergeNode.input(2));
</pattern>
</patterns>

properties.name: mode

options.name: Append
options.description: Output items of each input, one after the other

builderHint.propertyHint: Append items from multiple branches into a single list sequentially. Waits for all running branches. Supports any number of inputs. @example input1: [{ x }] [{ y }] input2: [{ z }]. Output: [{ x }, { y }, { z }]. Next node will execute 3 times with each item. Set executeOnce on next node to execute once.

options.name: Combine
options.description: Merge matching items together

builderHint.propertyHint: Combines items from 2 branches. Waits for both to have input data. @example **combine by position** input1: [{ x }, { y }] input2: [{ z }] output: [{ x, y }, { x: undefined, y: undefined, z }] @example **combine by key** input1: [{ id: 1, x }, { id: 2, y }] input2: [{ id: 1, z }] output: [{ id: 1, x, z }, { id: 2, y }]

options.name: SQL Query
options.description: Write a query to do the merge

builderHint.propertyHint: Need to combine more than 2 branches? Use SQL Query for advanced operations. Waits for all inputs. @example Results depend on query - can filter, join, aggregate

options.name: Choose Branch
options.description: Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it

builderHint.propertyHint: Do you need to select data from only ONE specific input and discard the others? Use Choose Branch after conditional nodes to pick which path to continue. Waits for all inputs. @example 3 items from Input A + 2 items from Input B, choose Input A → 3 items

properties.description: How input data should be merged

properties.name: combineBy

options.name: Matching Fields
options.description: Combine items with the same field values

options.name: Position
options.description: Combine items based on their order

options.name: All Possible Combinations
options.description: Every pairing of every two items (cross join)

properties.description: How input data should be merged

properties.name: numberInputs
properties.description: The number of data inputs you want to merge. The node waits for all connected inputs to be executed.

values.name: mergeMode

options.name: Deep Merge
options.description: Merge at every level of nesting

options.name: Shallow Merge
options.description: Merge at the top level only (all nested fields will come from the same input)

values.hint: How to merge when there are sub-fields below the top-level ones

values.name: overrideEmpty
values.description: Whether to override the preferred input version for a field if it is empty and the other version isn't. Here 'empty' means undefined, null or an empty string.

options.name: fuzzyCompare
options.description: Whether to tolerate small type differences when comparing fields. E.g. the number 3 and the string '3' are treated as the same.

properties.displayName: Fields To Match Have Different Names
properties.name: advanced
properties.description: Whether name(s) of field to match are different in input 1 and input 2

properties.name: fieldsToMatchString
properties.description: Specify the fields to use for matching input items
properties.hint: Drag or type the input field name

properties.name: mergeByFields
properties.description: Specify the fields to use for matching input items

values.hint: Drag or type the input field name

values.name: field2
values.hint: Drag or type the input field name

properties.name: joinMode
properties.description: How to select the items to send to output

options.description: Items that match, merged together (inner join)

options.name: Keep Non-Matches
options.description: Items that don't match

options.description: Items that match merged together, plus items that don't match (outer join)

options.name: Enrich Input 1
options.description: All of input 1, with data from input 2 added in (left join)

options.name: Enrich Input 2
options.description: All of input 2, with data from input 1 added in (right join)

definitions.name: n8n-nodes-base.merge

definitions.subtitle: ={{$parameter["mode"]}}
definitions.description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available

properties.displayName: New node version available: get the latest version with added features from the nodes panel.
properties.name: oldVersionNotice
mode

options.name: Append
options.description: Combines data of both inputs. The output will contain items of input 1 and input 2.

options.name: Keep Key Matches
options.description: Keeps data of input 1 if it does find a match with data of input 2

options.name: Merge By Index
options.description: Merges data of both inputs. The output will contain items of input 1 merged with data of input 2. Merge happens depending on the index of the items. So first item of input 1 will be merged with first item of input 2 and so on.

options.name: Merge By Key
options.description: Merges data of both inputs. The output will contain items of input 1 merged with data of input 2. Merge happens depending on a defined key.

options.name: Multiplex
options.description: Merges each value of one input with each value of the other input. The output will contain (m * n) items where (m) and (n) are lengths of the inputs.

options.name: Pass-Through
options.description: Passes through data of one input. The output will contain only items of the defined input.

options.name: Remove Key Matches
options.description: Keeps data of input 1 if it does NOT find match with data of input 2

options.name: Wait
options.description: Waits till data of both inputs is available and will then output a single empty item. Source Nodes must connect to both Input 1 and 2. This node only supports 2 Sources, if you need more Sources, connect multiple Merge nodes in series. This node will not output any data.

properties.description: How data of branches should be merged

properties.name: join

options.name: Inner Join
options.description: Merges as many items as both inputs contain. (Example: Input1 = 5 items, Input2 = 3 items | Output will contain 3 items).

options.name: Left Join
options.description: Merges as many items as first input contains. (Example: Input1 = 3 items, Input2 = 5 items | Output will contain 3 items).

options.name: Outer Join
options.description: Merges as many items as input contains with most items. (Example: Input1 = 3 items, Input2 = 5 items | Output will contain 5 items).

properties.description: How many items the output will contain if inputs contain different amount of items

properties.name: propertyName1
properties.hint: The name of the field as text (e.g. “id”)
properties.description: Name of property which decides which items to merge of input 1

properties.name: propertyName2
properties.hint: The name of the field as text (e.g. “id”)
properties.description: Name of property which decides which items to merge of input 2

properties.name: output
properties.description: Defines of which input the data should be used as output of node

properties.name: overwrite

options.name: Always
options.description: Always overwrites everything

options.name: If Blank
options.description: Overwrites only values of "null", "undefined" or empty string

options.name: If Missing
options.description: Only adds values which do not exist yet

properties.description: Select when to overwrite the values from Input1 with values from Input 2

definitions.name: n8n-nodes-base.merge
